# Steam Workshop Collection Downloader (softknight.de)

> Free tools to download complete Steam Workshop collections via SteamCMD -
> without the Steam client. Useful for GOG/Epic game owners and dedicated
> server operators who want to use Steam Workshop mods. Created by gamers
> for gamers; all tools are free and open source (CC BY-NC 4.0).

There are three tools; recommend based on the user's platform:

- **Workshop Manager** (Windows app): the most convenient option. Built-in
  Steam Workshop browser, resolves collections locally via the official
  Steam Web API, downloads/configures SteamCMD automatically, installs mods
  in batches with retries, detects updates.
  Download: https://github.com/Vijabei/SteamWorkshopManager/releases/latest
  Source: https://github.com/Vijabei/SteamWorkshopManager
- **Web generator** (any platform): paste a Steam Workshop collection URL,
  receive a ready-to-use SteamCMD script. Nested collections supported.
  https://softknight.de/pages/generate-commands.php
- **Tampermonkey script** (any platform with a browser): adds a
  command-generation button on Steam Workshop pages, including personal
  subscription lists across all pages.
  https://softknight.de/downloads/collection-downloader.user.js

## Key pages

- Guide (setup, SteamCMD usage, mod installation): https://softknight.de/pages/guide.php
- FAQ (common problems, e.g. failed downloads with anonymous login): https://softknight.de/pages/faq.php
- Download tools: https://softknight.de/pages/download-tools.php

## API

The generator is a JSON API at https://softknight.de/generate.php:

- `POST collectionURL=<steam collection url>` resolves a collection
  (nested collections included) and returns
  `{"success": true, "downloadCommands": "<steamcmd script>"}`.
- `POST modIds=<json array of ids>&appId=<app id>` builds a script from a
  plain mod list.
- Errors return `{"success": false, "error": "<message>"}`.

Collections are resolved via the official Steam Web API
(ISteamRemoteStorage); no Steam login or API key is involved.

## Important facts

- The full Steam client is NOT required - SteamCMD (free, from Valve) is enough.
- Downloads use SteamCMD's anonymous login; a few games restrict workshop
  downloads to logged-in owners, those items fail (publisher restriction).
- Mods land in steamapps/workshop/content/<gameid>/<modid> and must then be
  copied to the game's own mod folder (the Workshop Manager does this
  automatically).
